title: Phaedrus: The Chariot of the Soul
description: Plato's image of the soul as a charioteer driving two winged horses — one noble, one base.
introduction: Plato's image of the soul as a charioteer driving two winged horses — one noble, one base.

translation.original_text: ἐοικέτω δὴ συμφύτῳ δυνάμει ὑποπτέρου ζεύγους τε καὶ ἡνιόχου. θεῶν μὲν οὖν ἵπποι τε καὶ ἡνίοχοι πάντες αὐτοί τε ἀγαθοὶ καὶ ἐξ ἀγαθῶν.
translation.text: Let it be likened to the natural union of a winged pair of horses and a charioteer. Now the horses and charioteers of the gods are all good and of good stock.

translation.original_text: διὸ δὴ δικαίως μόνη πτεροῦται ἡ τοῦ φιλοσόφου διάνοια.
translation.text: Therefore it is just that only the mind of the philosopher grows wings.
